sanskrit:
सत्त्वात्संजायते ज्ञानं रजसो लोभ एव च।
प्रमादमोहौ तमसो भवतोऽज्ञानमेव च॥
transliteration: sattvāt sañjāyate jñānaṃ rajaso lobha eva ca | pramāda-mohau tamaso bhavato 'jñānam eva ca ||
literal_meaning: From sattva arises knowledge; from rajas, greed; from tamas, negligence and delusion, and ignorance indeed.
contextual_meaning: The mind’s dominant guna dictates whether we perceive clearly (sattva), crave compulsively (rajas), or dissociate into apathy & confusion (tamas).
psychological_state: Arjuna is being shown an internal map: every thought-emotion impulse can be traced to one of three neuro-affective modes, giving him a diagnostic lens for his own paralysis.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling late-night shopping apps, feeling the dopamine spike (rajas) turn into buyer’s remorse and mental fog (tamas) the next morning.
anchor_text: Notice which mood—clear, craving, or foggy—shapes your next thought.
